$Comment: Wave-2 (scope2-w2 leg A6, gate Q4/section 4.5 flagship exemplar): REPLACES the retired pack Stations/Sawmill.json full-file override. The jar's own Sawmill.json (RPG Stations, leg A5-reauthored) stays LIVE untouched, and this ExtensionAsset ADDS to it additively (Target.Station, section 1.8): a Loot ref to this pack's own SawmillLuckTiers lootable (see that file's own $Comment for the loot-side rebalance rationale). A8 REVIEW FIX (M1): the Xp payload this file originally carried ([{WOODCUTTING, PerCycle 8.0}]) was DROPPED. Because Work.Xp appends rather than replaces (section 1.8 rule 3, unkeyed array), and the jar's own Sawmill.json already authors Work.Xp [{WOODCUTTING 8.0},{CRAFTING 4.0}] (byte-identical to the pre-scope-2 pack total, per that file's $Comment), re-adding WOODCUTTING here DOUBLED the effective total to 16.0/cycle instead of adding a genuinely-new skill. The jar base already grants exactly the old pack total, so this extension now carries ONLY the genuinely-new Loot ref and the jar remains the single authority for the sawmill's WOODCUTTING/CRAFTING XP. To re-tune the sawmill's XP, edit the jar's Work.Xp (the extension mechanism intentionally cannot override the non-extensible Work group; an extension is for ADDING a new skill, never re-adding one the base already grants). The flagship-exemplar value survives: this file still demonstrates the ExtensionAsset mechanism additively extending a base station's Loot without a full-file override.
